hero.title: Rebalance Review
hero.subhead: A cleaner, PM-style view of current concentration, hidden factor overlap, and a proposed rebalance built for upside participation with tighter risk control.

summary: Single-name and regional concentration are too high for the portfolio’s stated drawdown-control objective.
Line-item count overstates diversification: AI/growth and China/HK exposures are more correlated than they look.
Defensive ballast exists, but not at a size that materially changes portfolio behavior in a risk-off tape.
The right move is not broad de-risking. It is a risk-budget reset toward higher-quality offense, less clutter, and better ballast.

stance.body: Not because of a hard bearish macro call. Because the current book has too much correlated factor exposure, too much capital in lower-efficiency positions, and not enough stabilizers relative to the cyclicality you’re carrying.

holdings.name: 9988.HK
holdings.rationale: Too large for a single China internet name in a mixed global book.

holdings.name: 2800.HK
holdings.rationale: Useful beta if intentional, but redundant at this size alongside 9988 and 9698.

holdings.name: TSLA
holdings.rationale: Too much volatility and narrative fragility for a double-digit weight.

holdings.name: MU
holdings.rationale: Keep cyclical memory exposure, but at a less aggressive size.

holdings.name: SPY
holdings.rationale: Use one broad index as the clean core beta sleeve.

holdings.name: 9698.HK
holdings.rationale: Keep only as smaller tactical China/AI infra torque.

holdings.name: MSFT
holdings.rationale: High-quality compounder and cleaner AI exposure than lower-quality alternatives.

holdings.name: 1816.HK
holdings.rationale: Defensive-ish China sleeve exposure, but keep modest.

holdings.name: INTC
holdings.rationale: Optionality exists, but weak use of scarce risk budget versus better names.

holdings.name: GLD
holdings.rationale: Real macro ballast that can change portfolio behavior in a risk-off tape.

holdings.name: AAPL
holdings.rationale: Useful quality stabilizer inside the tech sleeve.

holdings.name: XLU
holdings.rationale: Raise the defensive sleeve to a size that matters.

holdings.name: NVDA
holdings.rationale: Keep offensive exposure concentrated in higher-quality winners.

holdings.name: LNG
holdings.rationale: Adds macro diversification, but should not dominate the book.

holdings.name: VOO
holdings.rationale: Duplicate of the intended US broad-beta role.

holdings.name: NOK
holdings.rationale: Tactical rerating sleeve only, not a core holding.

holdings.name: Cash / dry powder
holdings.rationale: Keep flexibility to add on confirmation instead of chasing first spikes.

bucketDescriptions.US AI / growth: High upside sleeve. Keep it focused on the cleanest expressions of AI / quality growth, not every exciting story.
bucketDescriptions.China / HK: Meaningful upside potential, but position sizing must respect policy, sentiment, and geopolitical discount risk.
bucketDescriptions.Ballast: This sleeve exists to materially change outcomes in a wobble, not just look diversified on paper.
bucketDescriptions.Optionality / clutter: Lowest-efficiency risk budget. These positions tend to add noise more than portfolio quality.
bucketDescriptions.Broad beta: One clean core market exposure is enough. Duplication is not sophistication.

rules: No single regional sleeve above 25% without explicit top-down intent.
Ballast sleeve should be large enough to alter outcomes, not just optics.
One broad US beta instrument only.
deployment: Upgrade toward higher-quality offense: MSFT, NVDA, ETN, one broad index.
Keep a meaningful China sleeve, but bring it back into a defensible range.
Increase GLD and XLU enough to matter.
If adding NOK, treat it as a 3% tactical rerating sleeve, not a core position.
